decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces and evaluates an Xception–LSTM deep convolutional neural-network method for diagnosing childhood pneumonia from X-rays.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit model-development study with comparative performance results.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes and tests C19D-Net, a hybrid convolutional neural-network technique for diagnosing COVID-19 from chest X-rays.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicitly develops a new diagnostic model and reports classification performance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Builds, fine-tunes, and evaluates a VGG16-based deep CNN model for COVID-19 and viral-pneumonia diagnosis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit diagnostic-model development and performance evaluation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Although developing a neural-network model is stated as an aim, the abstract only reports conventional comparisons of clinical parameters and does not describe or evaluate a diagnostic or forecasting neural-network model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No explicit completed neural-network diagnostic or forecasting method is presented.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes a two-stage hybrid ARIMA–neural-network system for forecasting daily COVID-19 cases and evaluates its forecast errors.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of a neural-network forecasting methodology.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and tests iterative convolutional neural-network architectures for predicting COVID-19 status from chest CT images.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit diagnostic-model construction and performance testing.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and benchmarks CDC Net, a CNN model for diagnosing five chest diseases from X-rays.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicitly introduces and tests a novel multi-disease diagnostic model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies and compares six established transfer-learning CNN architectures for COVID-19 detection from CT images.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete COVID-19 diagnostic application of neural-network models rather than a clearly novel neural-network method.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses CNN-derived image features in a treatment recommender system, not in a diagnostic or disease-forecasting model for the assigned concept.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The neural network supports treatment-resource recommendation rather than the exact diagnostic/forecasting concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a novel genetic-algorithm-selected Xception neural network for COVID-19 diagnosis from X-rays.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit novel diagnostic-network development and benchmarking.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a CNN diagnostic model for H. pylori infection and validates it on internal and external endoscopy datasets.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit multicenter development and validation of a neural-network diagnostic model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Establishes and externally validates an artificial neural-network screening model for carbapenem-resistant K. pneumoniae.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of an ANN diagnostic model using MALDI-TOF spectra.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and externally validates a multimodal neural network predicting surgical-site infection within 48 hours.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of a neural-network infection-prediction model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and validates an NNET model for predicting multiple HPV co-infections from clinical and vaginal biomarkers.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit neural-network risk-prediction model development.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes and evaluates aiGeneR 3.0, an LSTM deep-network model for resistant-strain identification and multidrug-resistance prediction.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit new prediction-model development and validation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and externally validates a multi-region, multi-image CNN for diagnosing gastric H. pylori infection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of a new neural-network diagnostic architecture.
supported_label_variants: multi-region, multi-image Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model

decisions.relationship_evidence: Directly critiques administrative-code sepsis surveillance as vulnerable to ascertainment and coding bias and contrasts it with objective EHR clinical criteria.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Review provides explicit methodological guidance favoring objective clinical surveillance criteria.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses stochastic simulation to evaluate how alternative passive-surveillance observation and notification networks affect outbreak detection and control.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit performance evaluation of surveillance-network detection structures.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a non-homogeneous hidden Markov observation model that nowcasts influenza activity from incomplete early surveillance observations.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development for surveillance nowcasting and epidemic-state detection.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Observes workers’ physical contacts during simulated cleaning; it does not use or develop a surveillance observation-process or disease-detection model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The word observation refers to recording behavior, not the assigned methodological concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes a bivariate Gaussian Cox-process model jointly representing the observation process and recurrent infection events.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development of an observation-process model with Bayesian inference.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Estimates dengue force of infection from serial serosurveys and compares notification-to-infection ratios, but does not explicitly formulate or apply an observation-process or detection model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Notification undercapture is an empirical result rather than an explicit observation-process model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Fits an exponential growth model to critical-care admissions to estimate COVID-19 cases, without explicitly modeling the observation or case-detection process.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The abstract supports epidemic extrapolation, not the exact observation-process concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and applies an SEIR extension that explicitly models false-negative diagnostic tests and unascertained infections.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of a method representing imperfect case detection.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Simulation study evaluates the detection performance of a Bayesian hidden Markov model against testing alone across surveillance scenarios.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit benchmark of a probabilistic infection-detection model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces and simulation-tests a statistical method estimating the fraction of epidemic cases captured through investigation and contact tracing.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit new real-time reporting estimator with bias and precision assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Presents ChronoStrain, a Bayesian model that explicitly estimates strain presence, absence, and abundance trajectories from longitudinal observations.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development and benchmarking of a longitudinal microbial-detection model.
supported_label_variants: sequence quality- and time-aware Bayesian model

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies a Bayesian hidden Markov model in which diagnostic-service availability informs the TB case-detection process and under-reporting estimates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to childhood TB notification data in Ethiopia.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a theoretical approach for quantifying epidemiological error caused by multiple reporting and under-reporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological treatment of disease-reporting error.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops stochastic SIR models with constant, time-varying, and infection-source-dependent reporting processes and evaluates inferential bias.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development of epidemic reporting-process models.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a C. difficile transmission model to estimate community-case under-reporting and systematic hospital/community misclassification.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete infectious-disease application incorporating reporting and classification imperfections.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Extends SEIR to model test misclassification, selective testing, undetected infections, and under-reporting, with an accompanying R package.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit method development accompanied by the SEIRfansy software implementation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes an SEIR extension accounting for diagnostic false negatives and testing-selection bias and estimating unreported infections and deaths.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological source that also provides the SEIRfansy R package.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Fits a compartmental model to early COVID-19 data from 54 African countries and estimates country-specific ascertainment and reporting rates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete multi-country application estimating epidemic under-ascertainment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes an iteratively regularized optimization method for reconstructing time-varying COVID-19 case-reporting rates from epidemic data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit novel inference algorithm for a reporting-process parameter.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes a Bayesian hierarchical model integrating multiple outcomes while representing time-varying under-reporting and weekday-specific reporting delays.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development and simulation evaluation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a Bayesian transmission framework that infers infections and a daily reporting ratio from reported deaths while accounting for infection-to-death timing.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological framework for inference under incomplete reporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares Markov-switching zero-inflated and hurdle count models, including their assumptions about perfect detection and under-reporting, on simulations and chikungunya data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit benchmark of alternative observation/reporting model assumptions and predictive performance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a general statistical framework that infers pathogen-specific trends despite time-varying testing and noise in the surveillance observation process.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development demonstrated across several surveillance systems.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces reporting- and sampling-bias adjustments for estimating contagion potential from incomplete incidence and spatial-contact observations.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of corrections for partial observation, heterogeneous reporting, and sampling inconsistency.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes a two-stage time-series and Poisson-regression method for attributing ILI consultations and estimating relative pathogen under-reporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicitly presented inference method for partially observed pathogen contributions.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies Bayesian inference to online surveillance records to estimate influenza transmission parameters while compensating for observational bias and under-reporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete multi-season, multi-city influenza application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes a Bayesian stochastic SEIR framework designed for irregularly observed, aggregated, and under-reported epidemic data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development supported by simulations and Ebola data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops incidence-data catalytic models that jointly estimate dengue transmission intensity and case-reporting probability where seroprevalence data are unavailable.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit method for inference from under-detected case notifications.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops approximation-based MCMC methods for estimating epidemic under-reporting and benchmarks them against RJMCMC.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit new partial-observation inference methodology.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces a hidden Markov count model with a time-varying mechanism for estimating epidemic under-reporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit novel model for an unobserved epidemic process and incomplete reports.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Computationally evaluates how observation-function misspecification and neglected under-reporting bias EnKF state estimates and forecasts.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit performance and bias analysis of epidemic observation models.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a Bayesian line-list method jointly imputing missing onset dates and nowcasting epidemic curves and reproduction numbers under reporting delays.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit new inference method for incomplete and delayed surveillance observations.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces an N-mixture hierarchical disease-dynamics model treating reported cases and recoveries as binomially thinned observations of a hidden population.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit new hidden-population method for estimating under-reporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Presents a Bayesian framework integrating case notifications and seroprevalence to infer time-varying testing efficiency and under-reporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological framework combining complementary partial observations.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes stochastic-frontier epidemic models that estimate both reported and undocumented cases with geographically varying reporting rates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit model development for inference under case undercounting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a state-space inference framework with a modified UKF and an explicit infection-observation probability for under-reported notification records.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological treatment of an imperfect infection-observation process.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops an analytical framework quantifying uncertainty and information loss caused by under-reporting and reporting delays in epidemic curves.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodology for assessing partially observed surveillance data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Designs and validates a rescaled SIR model with an explicit parameter for the proportion of infections reported.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological modification for estimating epidemic under-reporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies Bayesian partial latent-class analysis to estimate individual and population pneumonia etiologies in the PERCH case-control study.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete multicountry pneumonia-etiology application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces the partially latent class model as an extension of latent-class models for estimating pneumonia etiologies from measurements with differing error rates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit foundational development, simulation testing, and application of the PLCM.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies a partial latent-class Bayesian model to estimate pathogen-specific etiologies of neonatal illness in the ANISA cohort.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete infectious-etiology application in Bangladesh, India, and Pakistan.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses registry reports and distributed vaccine doses as the denominator to calculate passive AEFI reporting rates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete vaccine-safety surveillance application in Slovenia.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Directly identifies unclear exposure denominators, duplicate cases, and interpretability limitations in pharmacovigilance-database safety analyses.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Empirical assessment diagnoses why passive-database reports cannot yield interpretable rates.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and externally validates a GBS risk-prediction model from passive VAERS adverse-event reports.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development for passive-surveillance risk prediction.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Surveys health professionals’ knowledge and attitudes to ADR reporting to identify weaknesses and potential causes of pharmacovigilance under-reporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit evaluation of adverse-event reporting practices.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses focus groups to evaluate ADR-detection and reporting practices after introduction of an ART pharmacovigilance plan.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit assessment of pharmacovigilance knowledge, barriers, and under-reporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies disproportionality analysis to VigiBase reports to assess infection signals associated with DPP-4 inhibitors.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete adverse-event signal analysis involving reported infections.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Establishes and operates an enhanced spontaneous AEFI surveillance system during a meningococcal-vaccine campaign.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit operational implementation across all district health centers.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses FDA adverse-event reports and reporting odds ratios to detect hepatitis-B safety signals for antirheumatic drugs.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete pharmacovigilance signal application involving HBV reactivation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a four-step planning method for sequential database safety surveillance that balances signal-detection timeliness, power, and database configuration.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological and design guidance for medical-product surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Systematically reviews and classifies VAERS hypersensitivity reports and estimates reporting rates per vaccine doses distributed.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete influenza-vaccine adverse-event surveillance application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Pilots targeted spontaneous reporting for renal toxicity as a complement to conventional ADR reporting in two Ugandan facilities.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit implementation of a targeted pharmacovigilance reporting system.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates feasibility, acceptance, and user preferences for an app prototype intended to supplement passive AEFI reporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit feasibility assessment of a proposed adverse-event surveillance implementation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses reports directed through the French Pharmacovigilance Network to analyze cardiac ADRs associated with off-label COVID-19 treatments.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete COVID-19 pharmacovigilance application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Reviews AEFI-surveillance tools and directly discusses under-reporting, rare-event detection, and system-response challenges.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit overview offering surveillance-system guidance and identifying limitations.
supported_label_variants: surveillance of adverse events following immunization

decisions.relationship_evidence: Explains why spontaneous-report counts and reporting rates cannot be interpreted as incidence without clinical review, background rates, and follow-up studies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological guidance on interpreting pharmacovigilance reports and signals.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates the feasibility and signal-detection performance of tree-temporal scan analysis in nationwide vaccine-safety data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit empirical validation of a safety-surveillance data-mining method.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies disproportionality analysis to WHO spontaneous safety reports to evaluate bacterial and herpesvirus infection signals for biologics.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete pharmacovigilance application to infectious adverse outcomes.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzes French and worldwide pharmacovigilance reports to characterize adverse events associated with HIV pre-exposure prophylaxis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete antiretroviral-safety surveillance application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and evaluates VAEM-Mine, an NLP method for extracting vaccine adverse-event mentions from Twitter for safety-signal monitoring.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of a complementary social-media adverse-event surveillance method.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Randomized trial evaluates the efficacy and feasibility of SMS and telephone-based active AEFI detection against passive reporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit comparative performance evaluation of adverse-event surveillance approaches.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Implements and evaluates a pharmacovigilance training and mentoring project across 91 facilities in Côte d’Ivoire.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit operational intervention that increased adverse-event reporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies descriptive and disproportionality analyses to EudraVigilance reports of antibiotic-associated C. difficile infection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete pharmacovigilance analysis of an infectious adverse event.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Implements VigiVax SMS-based active AEFI surveillance integrated with immunization registries and the Italian Pharmacovigilance system.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit digital surveillance implementation with automated report submission.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates whether South Africa’s spontaneous AEFI system detects disproportionate-reporting signals and identifies under-reporting and masking.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit surveillance-system performance evaluation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Conducts prospective multicenter surveillance within a transplant-recipient cohort, but does not describe population-based catchment surveillance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The denominator is participating centers’ transplant recipients rather than an explicitly population-based surveillance population.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses active population-based surveillance with metropolitan catchment populations as denominators to estimate MRSA incidence trends.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete population-based incidence-surveillance application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses national case reports and hospital-consultation denominators to estimate condition-specific risk, not an explicitly population-based incidence-surveillance design.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The abstract supports administrative surveillance analysis rather than the exact assigned design.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzes incidence in an insured claims cohort using enrollment denominators but does not explicitly use population-based incidence surveillance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: A claims-database incidence analysis is not itself proof of the assigned surveillance design.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses population- and laboratory-based data with population denominators to estimate invasive pneumococcal disease incidence before and after vaccine introduction.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete population-based infectious-disease incidence analysis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Active Bacterial Core surveillance, explicitly described as population-based, with catchment population denominators to estimate iGAS incidence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application of population-based incidence surveillance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes a two-stage hierarchical Bayesian geostatistical model that adjusts veterinary parasite-risk inference for preferential sampling.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit preferential-sampling model development and application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces an adaptive preferential-sampling phylodynamic model with a time-varying relationship between sampling rate and effective population size.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development implemented in the adapref R package.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces and benchmarks an adaptive preferential-sampling model that jointly represents viral genealogy and a time-varying sampling process.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit method source with an available adapref R-package implementation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Presents a preferential-sampling phylodynamic method that incorporates historical sample-to-report delays into the sampling model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit new method for correcting real-time phylodynamic estimates.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Defines public-health triangulation and lays out a 12-step process for synthesizing multiple secondary data sources for decisions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological guidance source describing the approach, limitations, and workflow.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses triangulation of programme, field, and surveillance data to assess HIV-intervention performance, coverage, and costs in Pakistan.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HIV-program application of data triangulation.
supported_label_variants: triangulation of programme, field and surveillance data

decisions.relationship_evidence: Triangulates program registries, hospital outcomes, census estimates, and survey data to assess Swaziland’s HIV response.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete national HIV/TB public-health triangulation application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses findings from a data-triangulation exercise to estimate South African GBMSM HIV treatment-cascade indicators under alternative scenarios.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HIV-program application of data triangulation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces and rolls out an Excel-based process for triangulating immunization-program and vaccine-supply data in two Ethiopian regions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit implementation and feasibility assessment of the IDTT process and tool.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Describes implemented data-triangulation processes, indicator prioritization, and capacity-building in immunization programs across three countries.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit multicountry operationalization of triangulation for surveillance decision-making.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Designs, implements, and delivers a hospital-onset COVID-19 surveillance system providing daily real-time network analyses of patient pathways.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit implementation of a novel real-time network-surveillance system.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Validates self-reported drug use against urinalysis and quantifies sensitivity and possible under-reporting in a household survey.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit measurement-validation study using an objective comparator.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Validates parent-reported vaccination histories against provider records and quantifies false positives, false negatives, kappa, and net reporting bias.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit surveillance-measurement validation and bias assessment.
